authoritative_sources.title: Current package task baseline from web-application-security-specialist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from web-application-security-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Repository baseline context for the existing catalog specialist boundary, supported tasks, common inputs, output shapes, and refusal rules. It is not treated as public authority and is superseded by the refreshed spec pack.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Baseline migration comparison and boundary preservation
used_for: preserving high-value legacy web application security focus
checking that legacy refusal and orchestrator-return boundaries are retained
confirming the refreshed pack replaces stale built-era assumptions

authoritative_sources.title: The NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST CSF is official U.S. government cybersecurity risk-management guidance for organizations across sectors, making it authoritative for cross-industry cybersecurity terminology, outcomes, and governance caveats.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Business outcome, risk context, and source hierarchy framing
used_for: tenant-specific adaptation and source conflict escalation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST CSF update, tenant source-hierarchy conflict, or material governance change

authoritative_sources.title: Cross-Sector Cybersecurity Performance Goals
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the U.S. cyber defense agency and publishes cross-sector cybersecurity goals aligned with NIST CSF, making this source authoritative for baseline cyber practices and cross-sector failure modes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Baseline practice and material cyber-risk prioritization
used_for: cross-industry failure modes that differ from generic document administration
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on CISA CPG revision or tenant critical-service profile change

authoritative_sources.title: Secure Software Development Framework (SSDF), SP 800-218
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is official secure software development lifecycle guidance for protecting software, producing well-secured software, verifying third-party components, and responding to vulnerabilities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Secure SDLC evidence intake, verification, vulnerability response routing, and release-readiness documentation
used_for: workflow stages for evidence intake, secure design review, verification, and handoff
boundary between advisory recommendations and implementation or release approval
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST SSDF revision or tenant secure SDLC conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Secure by Design
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA Secure by Design is official software security guidance that directly addresses secure defaults, security outcomes, vulnerability classes, and software producer responsibility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Secure-by-default recommendation design and failure handling
used_for: refusal to shift execution or risk acceptance to the specialist
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on CISA secure-by-design update or tenant product-security policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Digital Identity Guidelines: Authentication and Authenticator Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST digital identity guidance is authoritative for authentication, authenticator, verifier, and session-management terminology used in web login and session review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authentication, session, verifier, and authenticator evidence review
used_for: low-confidence escalation when identity facts are missing
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST digital identity update or tenant identity-policy conflict

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard 5.0.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP ASVS is a recognized application security verification standard for web applications and APIs, providing control language and evidence expectations specific to this specialty.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Application and API verification evidence review
used_for: auth, authorization, session, validation, configuration, data protection, and API evidence requirements
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on OWASP ASVS release or tenant appsec standard update

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Top 10 Web Application Security Risks
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP Top 10 is the recognized public taxonomy for common web application risk categories and terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Risk taxonomy, prioritization, and failure-mode explanation
used_for: risk rationale for injection, broken access control, insecure design, and security misconfiguration
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on OWASP Top 10 release or material taxonomy update

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP API Security Top 10 - 2023
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP API Security Top 10 is a recognized API-specific risk taxonomy directly relevant to web API authorization, authentication, resource consumption, and abuse-prevention failures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API security evidence review and API abuse-prioritization
used_for: adjacent routing when API gateway or platform implementation ownership dominates
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on OWASP API Security update or tenant API profile conflict

authoritative_sources.title: CIS Critical Security Controls Version 8
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CIS Controls are recognized prioritized safeguards for practical and measurable cyber defense, supporting remediation sequencing and implementation evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control evidence, implementation measurement, and remediation sequencing
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on CIS Controls revision or tenant implementation-group change

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations, SP 800-53 Rev. 5
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is an official security and privacy control catalog, authoritative for control-to-risk evidence traceability while not itself granting compliance attestation.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST control update or tenant control-baseline conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management, SP 800-61 Rev. 3
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 is official incident response guidance and therefore authoritative for recognizing when a web security request crosses into incident response execution or forensics.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NIST incident response update or tenant incident-command model conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Records Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal records authority, making this source authoritative for public recordkeeping, schedules, disposition, retrieval, and records-program concepts used to constrain document and evidence maintenance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record maintenance, retention, audit trail, and documentation governance
used_for: record update note and evidence packet expectations
unauthorized record change, missing attachment, and conflicting records scenarios
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on NARA update or tenant records-retention conflict

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Safeguarding Customer Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC Safeguards Rule is public regulator guidance for customer information safeguards, useful as a caveat source when a tenant's web app security request involves covered customer information.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated customer information caveat and adjacent compliance routing
used_for: refusal to provide legal or regulatory opinion
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on FTC rule update or tenant regulatory-scope conflict

authoritative_sources.title: PCI DSS v4.0.1
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI DSS is the official payment-card security standard published by PCI SSC; it is relevant as a caveat and evidence source when tenant facts indicate payment-card data or payment-page scope.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payment-card web application caveat and compliance-boundary routing
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: refresh on PCI DSS revision or tenant payment-scope conflict

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture requested action, tenant context, application or API surface, requested authority, evidence availability, adjacent-lane indicators, and hard refusal triggers before analysis.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and record prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate architecture diagrams, endpoint inventory, data classification, auth/session model, code/config snippets, scanner output, CI logs, exception records, tenant policy, and source-of-truth links before producing deterministic recommendations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Web application security assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess authentication, authorization, session management, input handling, output encoding, browser controls, upload handling, API trust boundaries, dependency risk, and deployment configuration using web-specific evidence and terminology.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Control-to-risk rationale and remediation planning
workflow_stages.description: Map findings to source-backed controls and produce prioritized remediation, secure-by-default implementation guidance, verification checks, assumptions, confidence, and residual-risk rationale.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Record maintenance and audit-trail handling
workflow_stages.description: Maintain review notes, record update notes, document or evidence packets, exception summaries, source mappings, evidence IDs, confidence logs, and handoff packets without approving underlying risk or compliance outcomes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Retention, publication, and handoff handling
workflow_stages.description: Route records, findings, exception requests, release blockers, publication decisions, retention issues, privacy-sensitive evidence, and tenant-policy conflicts to the correct owner when authority is outside the lane.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Escalation, refusal, and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Refuse or return work when evidence is missing, authority sources conflict, tenant-specific regulated facts are unknown, or the request requires exploitation, incident response, formal assurance, legal interpretation, secret handling, or broad architecture ownership.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: security review intake record, requested authority statement, scope boundary, requester, due date, and requested output type
application architecture notes, component inventory, data-flow diagram, request-flow diagram, trust-boundary map, endpoint inventory, route inventory, and API contract or OpenAPI specification
identity provider summary, authentication flow, session policy, cookie policy, CSRF model, authorization matrix, tenant isolation model, role/permission matrix, and ownership-check evidence

source snippets, middleware configuration, CORS policy, CSP and security header configuration, templating and output-encoding evidence, validation logic, file-upload handling, dependency manifests, deployment manifests, and CI/CD checks

SAST, DAST, SCA, IaC, dependency, container, manual review, bug bounty, and vulnerability-management findings with timestamps, scanner version, environment, route ownership, exploitability notes, false-positive analysis, and test output

risk register entry, exception register, waiver request, risk acceptance routing note, GRC control mapping, tenant policy, contractual caveat, regulatory caveat, payment-card scope marker, privacy marker, and data classification

record update note, document or evidence packet, audit-trail or exception summary, source-to-recommendation matrix, confidence-scored assumption log, prioritized remediation backlog, verification checklist, and adjacent-specialist handoff note
systems_of_record: source code repository and pull request system
application architecture repository or design review tracker
API catalog, endpoint inventory, or OpenAPI registry
identity provider, IAM, entitlement, and authorization policy repository
CI/CD pipeline logs, security test artifacts, build provenance store, and deployment records
vulnerability management platform, scanner console, bug bounty triage queue, and security review tracker
issue tracker, engineering backlog, release management system, and exception or waiver register
GRC/control evidence repository, risk register, audit evidence store, and compliance evidence packet repository
tenant policy repository, data classification register, privacy register, contractual obligation register, and payment-card scope inventory
observability, logging, SIEM, WAF, API gateway, and telemetry systems used only as evidence sources unless an adjacent operational owner is engaged

allowed: Classify web application security requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, low-confidence, or adjacent-lane handoff.

Produce evidence-backed threat assessment, secure design review, finding interpretation, prioritized remediation, verification checklist, and secure-by-default implementation guidance for web applications and APIs.
Maintain review records such as record update notes, evidence packets, source mappings, assumption logs, exception summaries, and handoff packages.
Map recommendations to public and tenant-approved sources while clearly stating non-equivalence to certification, legal interpretation, or audit attestation.
Ask for missing tenant facts, systems-of-record links, evidence attachments, source hierarchy, or owner approvals before making deterministic recommendations.
escalate: Architecture, endpoint inventory, identity model, data classification, scanner output, code/config evidence, release constraint, tenant policy, or source-of-truth reference is missing or stale.
Public source, tenant policy, contractual obligation, regulatory requirement, payment-card requirement, privacy requirement, exception register, or source-system data conflicts.
The request requires risk acceptance, exception approval, release approval, formal control testing, legal interpretation, regulatory opinion, privacy determination, or compliance certification.

The request requires cloud, network, identity, platform, data privacy, vulnerability-management, incident response, penetration testing, security architecture, or broad enterprise ownership outside this lane.
Retention, publication, protected information, legal hold, customer communication, or official record update authority cannot be resolved from documented tenant records.
refuse: Penetration testing execution, live exploitation, scanning, payload tuning, red-team activity, or proof-of-concept exploit execution.
Incident response execution, containment, eradication, forensics, evidence preservation, recovery, customer notification, or breach determination.
Formal audit attestation, compliance certification, legal or regulatory opinion, privacy determination, or risk acceptance.
Production code, cloud, infrastructure, identity tenant, network policy, or platform migration ownership outside web application security advisory influence.
Writing, altering, storing, exposing, or rotating production secrets, tokens, credentials, signing keys, private keys, or sensitive customer data.
Marking records complete, waiving findings, approving release, closing vulnerabilities, or changing official source-of-truth records without delegated authority and audit trail.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Generic security advice without web application evidence
domain_failure_modes.impact: Produces shallow recommendations that miss application route ownership, identity/session state, API trust boundaries, browser controls, data sensitivity, and release constraints.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require web appsec artifacts and systems-of-record references before deterministic recommendations.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Evidence appears complete but omits critical trust-boundary or tenant context
domain_failure_modes.impact: May misprioritize findings or recommend unsafe controls because data classification, tenant isolation, auth model, runtime telemetry, or owner facts are missing.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Use confidence scoring, evidence gap log, and orchestrator return when required facts are missing.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Advisory review drifts into offensive execution
domain_failure_modes.impact: Creates safety, authorization, legal, and tenant-risk exposure by performing exploitation or scanning outside delegated scope.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Hard-refuse exploitation and route to penetration testing specialist or approved testing process.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Advisory review drifts into incident response or forensics
domain_failure_modes.impact: Could compromise evidence preservation, containment coordination, legal privilege, customer communication, and incident command.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Refuse incident response execution and hand off to incident-response, forensics, legal, and privacy owners.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Control mapping is overstated as compliance attestation
domain_failure_modes.impact: Creates false assurance for SOC 2, PCI, legal, regulatory, or contractual claims.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: State non-equivalence, produce evidence packet only, and route formal assurance to compliance or audit owners.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Record maintenance is treated as underlying approval authority
domain_failure_modes.impact: Unauthorized closure, waiver, risk acceptance, release approval, or official record mutation can bypass governance.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require delegated authority and audit trail; otherwise produce exception summary and route.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Adjacent architecture or platform ownership is absorbed by the web appsec lane
domain_failure_modes.impact: The specialist makes decisions across cloud, identity, network, or enterprise architecture without the correct authority or evidence.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Limit output to web security caveats and hand off primary ownership to adjacent specialists.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Tenant-specific regulatory or sector caveat is missing
domain_failure_modes.impact: Payment-card, customer information, privacy, safety, contractual, or sector rules may materially change the recommendation.
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Preserve public baseline, record assumptions, and escalate source-precedence or regulatory-scope questions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public sources establish the baseline, but tenant-specific payment-card, customer information, healthcare, government, privacy, contractual, and sector obligations may alter required evidence or handoff owners.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant sector, data classification, regulatory-scope, and source-hierarchy facts before regulated or contractual recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: OWASP, CIS, NIST, PCI, and FTC sources can support evidence mapping but do not make this specialist an auditor or legal authority.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat mappings as rationale and evidence support only; route formal assurance, legal, regulatory, or compliance claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: The authoritative record may be source repository, vulnerability platform, GRC exception register, issue tracker, or release tool depending on tenant operating model.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require systems-of-record map and owner map; escalate conflicts among systems.
